mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal savings (RSAFS) indicate stronger household balance sheets, supporting consumption spending and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with RSAFS growth rates over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.25%, the market prices in a shift from accommodative to neutral policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25% across multiple tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and rises in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal supply chain bottlenecks coinciding with labor market softening, which compresses corporate margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling INDPRO and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where unemployment falls below 5.0%, rapid payroll growth (PAYEMS) triggers wage-push inflation expectations, leading the Fed to hike rates preemptively and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly negative when PAYEMS growth exceeds 300k monthly and UNRATE remains below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong consumer confidence and credit availability, which supports durable goods manufacturing and retail earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive relationship with HOUST month-over-month changes over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 235.0, inflation expectations become unanchored, prompting equity risk premium expansion and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.0 across multiple inflationary episodes.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of strong GDP growth (GDPC1) and rising real savings (RSAFS) indicates robust demand without savings depletion, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, rapid rebounds in industrial production (INDPRO) outpace labor market recovery, easing supply constraints and boosting profit margins, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are not significantly positive when INDPRO exceeds 105.0 during post-shock recovery periods.
